title: sambucus nigra subsp. maderensis
intro: Sambucus nigra subsp. maderensis, also known as the Madeiran elderberry, is a subspecies of the European elderberry plant, belonging to the honeysuckle family. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Madeiran elderberry include reducing symptoms of colds and flu, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Sambucus nigra subsp. maderensis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
